summary: HOTFIX VALIDATED: Accounts section crash ('accounts.map is not a function') is fixed. Backend API /api/v1/admin/accounts-with-users returns correct format with 'accounts' key, pagination object, and camelCase fields. Frontend renders 74 accounts correctly with pagination.

hotfix_validation.Frontend loads without crash: PASS - No 'accounts.map is not a function' error
hotfix_validation.Frontend pagination works: PASS - Page 1 of 2, Next/Last buttons visible

critical_code_review_comments: Backend response format correctly changed from {data: [...]} to {accounts: [...]} matching frontend expectations
Added 'limit' query param support for frontend compatibility
Fields correctly use camelCase (userName, userEmail, userId) for frontend binding
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hotfix for 'accounts.map is not a function' validated. Backend /api/v1/admin/accounts-with-users now returns {accounts: [...], pagination: {...}} format. Frontend AdminAccountsControl.js correctly renders 74 accounts with pagination.
rca_of_issue: Original bug: Backend returned {data: [...]} but frontend expected {accounts: [...]}. This caused accounts.map() to fail because 'accounts' was undefined. Fixed by: 1) Changed response key from 'data' to 'accounts', 2) Added 'limit' param alias for 'page_size', 3) Used camelCase field names (userName, userEmail, userId) matching frontend bindings.
